input.initialPrompt:
I'm on a headless Linux CI server and need to build my MAUI app for Android.
I have the .NET SDK installed but nothing else.
Give me a specific plan to set up the environment, including how to find
the correct versions for everything.

data.content:
Here's a concrete plan for building a MAUI Android app on headless Linux:

## 1. Install the MAUI workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui-android
```

## 2. Find required Android SDK versions

Check what your project needs:

```bash
# Look at your .csproj for TargetFramework (e.g., net8.0-android34)
grep -i "TargetFramework" *.csproj

# The number after "android" is the compile SDK version (e.g., 34)
```

## 3. Install Android SDK command-line tools

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/android-sdk"
mkdir -p "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools"

# Download command-line tools (check https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only for latest)
wget -q https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-linux-11076708_latest.zip -O /tmp/cmdtools.zip
unzip -q /tmp/cmdtools.zip -d "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools"
mv "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/cmdline-tools" "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest"
```

## 4. Accept licenses & install packages

```bash
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH"

yes | sdkmanager --licenses

# Install what MAUI needs (match API level to your TargetFramework):
sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0"
```

## 5. Find exact build-tools version needed

```bash
# List available build-tools to pick the right one:
sdkmanager --list | grep build-tools

# Check MAUI workload manifest for its expected versions:
dotnet workload search --version
cat $(dotnet --list-sdks | awk '{print $2}' | tail -1)/sdk-manifests/*/microsoft.net.sdk.android/WorkloadManifest.json 2>/dev/null | grep -i version
```

## 6. Install OpenJDK 17

```bash
# MAUI/Android Gradle requires JDK 17
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y openjdk-17-jdk
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
```

## 7. Build

```bash
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -c Release
# or to produce an APK/AAB:
dotnet publish -f net8.0-android -c Release
```

## Key version-matching rules

| Component | How to determine version |
|-----------|------------------------|
| Android API level | From `<TargetFramework>` in .csproj (`net8.0-android34` → API 34) |
| Build-tools | Match major version to API level (e.g., `34.x.x` for API 34) |
| JDK | JDK 17 for .NET 8+/MAUI; check [MS docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation) |
| .NET MAUI workload | Must match your .NET SDK major version (`dotnet --version`) |

## Quick verification

```bash
dotnet workload list          # confirm maui-android installed
sdkmanager --list_installed   # confirm Android packages
java -version                 # confirm JDK 17
```

This is the minimum setup — no emulator or Android Studio needed for CI builds.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a specific plan to set up a headless Linux CI environment for building a .NET MAUI app for Android. They have the .NET SDK installed but need everything else. Let me provide a concise, actionable plan.
